ASH Security Scan Results

Project: ASH

Scan executed: 2026-02-23T22:49:10+00:00

Report generated: 2026-02-23T22:51:35+00:00

ASH version: 3.2.1

Scanner Results

How to read scanner results

  • Severity levels:
    • Suppressed (S): Findings that have been explicitly suppressed and don't affect scanner status
    • Critical (C): Highest severity findings that require immediate attention
    • High (H): Serious findings that should be addressed soon
    • Medium (M): Moderate risk findings
    • Low (L): Lower risk findings
    • Info (I): Informational findings with minimal risk
  • Duration: Time taken by the scanner to complete its execution
  • Actionable: Number of findings at or above the threshold severity level that require attention
  • Result:
    • PASSED = No findings at or above threshold
    • FAILED = Findings at or above threshold
    • MISSING = Required dependencies not available
    • SKIPPED = Scanner explicitly disabled
    • ERROR = Scanner execution error
  • Threshold: The minimum severity level that will cause a scanner to fail
  • Statistics calculation:
    • All statistics are calculated from the final aggregated SARIF report
    • Suppressed findings are counted separately and do not contribute to actionable findings
    • Scanner status is determined by comparing actionable findings to the threshold

ERROR python.sqlalchemy.security.sqlalchemy-execute-raw-query.sqlalchemy-execute-raw-query Avoiding SQL string concatenation: untrusted input concatenated with raw SQL query can result in SQL Injection. In order to execute raw query safely, prepared statement should be used. SQLAlchemy provides TextualSQL to easily used prepared statement with named parameters. For complex SQL composition, use SQL Expression Language or Schema Definition Language. In most cases, SQLAlchemy ORM will be a better option. deployment/database/secrets-rotation/src/function/rotate-secrets.py:201

This may lead to secret credentials being exposed. Make sure that the logger is not logging sensitive information. deployment/database/secrets-rotation/src/function/rotate-secrets.py:203
ERROR python.lang.security.audit.logging.logger-credential-leak.python-logger-credential-disclosure Detected a python logger call with a potential hardcoded secret "testSecret: Successfully signed into MySQL DB with AWSPENDING secret in %s." % arn being logged. ERROR yaml.docker-compose.security.writable-filesystem-service.writable-filesystem-service Service 'orders-db' is running with a writable root filesystem. This may allow malicious applications to download and run additional payloads, or modify container files. If an application inside a container has to save something temporarily consider using a tmpfs. Add 'read_only: true' to this service to prevent this. source/orders/docker-compose.yml:44
ERROR yaml.docker-compose.security.writable-filesystem-service.writable-filesystem-service Service 'rabbitmq' is running with a writable root filesystem. This may allow malicious applications to download and run additional payloads, or modify container files. If an application inside a container has to save something temporarily consider using a tmpfs. Add 'read_only: true' to this service to prevent this. source/orders/docker-compose.yml:66

ERROR html.security.audit.missing-integrity.missing-integrity This tag is missing an 'integrity' subresource integrity attribute. The 'integrity' attribute allows for the browser to verify that externally hosted files (for example from a CDN) are delivered without unexpected manipulation. Without this attribute, if an attacker can modify the externally hosted resource, this could lead to XSS and other types of attacks. To prevent this, include the base64-encoded cryptographic hash of the resource (file) you’re telling the browser to fetch in the 'integrity' attribute for all externally hosted files. source/ui/src/main/resources/templates/fragments/error.html:8
